title: paspalum clandestinum
intro: Paspalum clandestinum, also known as Kuka or Kukab, is a type of tropical grass native to the Pacific Islands and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including treating skin conditions, wounds, and infections, as well as reducing fever and promoting digestion. In herbal preparations, Paspalum clandestinum is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, allowing its medicinal properties to be utilized in a range of applications.
